Reasoning: The patient's current hospital visit record is dominated by severe, acute, and complex infectious and inflammatory processes, as well as severe trauma (closed fracture of the femur). The primary diagnoses are: Closed fracture of subtrochanteric section of neck of femur; Chronic lymphoid leukemia, without mention of having achieved remission; Pneumonia due to Pseudomonas; Pneumonia in other systemic mycoses; Autoimmune hemolytic anemias; Bacteremia; Hypogammaglobulinemia, unspecified; Acute bronchiolitis due to respiratory syncytial virus (RSV); Delirium due to conditions classified elsewhere; Other and unspecified mycoses; Other candidiasis of other specified sites; Peptic ulcer of unspecified site, unspecified as acute or chronic, without mention of hemorrhage or perforation, without mention of obstruction; Congestive heart failure, unspecified; Streptococcus infection in conditions classified elsewhere and of unspecified site, other streptococcus; Coronary atherosclerosis of native coronary artery; Hypercalcemia; Other diseases of lung, not elsewhere classified; Personal history of other malignant neoplasm of skin; Long-term (current) use of steroids; Accidental fall from bed.
